summary: Comprehensive testing of Employee Reactivation feature. All 11 backend tests passed (100%). Frontend UI verified: Activate button (UserCheck icon) shows for inactive employees, Deactivation Details section displays in Profile tab with data-testid='inactive-details', Reactivate Employee button visible with data-testid='reactivate-btn'. Active employees show Deactivate (Trash) button. Payroll weekoff calculation correctly counts only up to inactive_date.

features_tested.reactivate_preserves_history: PASS - inactive_type and inactive_date preserved after reactivation
features_tested.reactivate_enables_user_login: PASS - User's is_active set to True in users collection
features_tested.reactivate_already_active_400: PASS - Returns 400 for already-active employee with message 'Employee is not inactive'
features_tested.filter_inactive_employees: PASS - GET /api/employees?status=Inactive returns only inactive employees
features_tested.payroll_weekoff_correction: PASS - weekoff_pay only counts Sundays/holidays up to inactive_date
features_tested.frontend_activate_button: PASS - Inactive employees show green UserCheck (Activate) button in table
features_tested.frontend_deactivate_button: PASS - Active employees show red Trash (Deactivate) button in table
features_tested.frontend_inactive_details_section: PASS - Profile tab shows 'DEACTIVATION DETAILS' section with data-testid='inactive-details'
features_tested.frontend_inactive_details_content: PASS - Section displays inactive_type, inactive_date, reason, last_day_payable
features_tested.frontend_reactivate_button: PASS - 'Reactivate Employee' button visible in detail view with data-testid='reactivate-btn'

critical_code_review_comments: Backend reactivate_employee endpoint correctly implemented at line 3250-3283 in server.py
Endpoint properly checks HR role (403), employee existence (404), and inactive status (400)
Reactivation preserves deactivation history (inactive_type, inactive_date, reason) - not cleared
User login re-enabled by setting is_active=True in users collection
Team member count incremented on reactivation
Frontend Employees.js correctly shows conditional Activate/Deactivate button based on employee_status
Deactivation Details section has proper data-testid='inactive-details' for testing
Reactivate button has proper data-testid='reactivate-btn' for testing
seed_data_creation: No new seed data created. Used existing TEST_Relieved_05658888 (id=employee-onboard-7) with inactive_date=2026-04-16.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Employee Reactivation feature fully tested. PUT /api/employees/{id}/reactivate endpoint works correctly. Frontend shows Activate button (UserCheck icon) for inactive employees and Deactivate button (Trash icon) for active employees. Employee detail Profile tab shows 'DEACTIVATION DETAILS' section with data-testid='inactive-details' containing inactive_type, inactive_date, reason, last_day_payable. Reactivate Employee button has data-testid='reactivate-btn'. Test employee TEST_Relieved_05658888 is currently Inactive with inactive_date=2026-04-16. Test file: /app/backend/tests/test_employee_reactivation.py
